    25‚Üí

    27‚Üí- [x] Both problems use the `theorem` keyword.
    28‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_919` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    29‚Üí- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
    30‚Üí- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
    31‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).

    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí### `answer()` usage

    36‚Üí- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`.
    37‚Üí- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (existence of such a graph).
    38‚Üí- [x] Quantifiers (the `‚ąÉ`) are on the RHS of `‚ÜĒ`, **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí## 7. Category Attribute
    41‚Üí- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.

    49‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.

    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíAll other checklist items pass. The formalization is well-structured, the `answer()` usage correctly matches the yes/no phrasing of the problem, the variant is properly named and documented, and the module docstring provides good mathematical context with a bibliography entry.

# Review: deepmind/919.lean

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 919`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/919](https://www.erdosproblems.com/919)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Babai's theorem, ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal counterexample, generalization question).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results in chromatic number theory_.`

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos919 ... end Erdos919`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos919`.
- [x] `open` statement (`open SimpleGraph Cardinal Ordinal`) appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions
- N/A ‚ÄĒ no helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Both problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_919` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstrings reference the source paper `[Er69b]`.

### `answer()` usage
The problem asks a yes/no question: "Is there a graph on a well-ordered set ‚Ä¶ ?" ‚ÄĒ this is **Case 1**.
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (existence of such a graph).
- [x] Quantifiers (the `‚ąÉ`) are on the RHS of `‚ÜĒ`, **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚ÄĒ a valid category.
- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (two: `5` and `3`).
- [ ] **FAIL: Numbers are NOT in ascending numeric order.** Both theorems use `AMS 5 3`; should be `AMS 3 5`.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `3` (Mathematical logic and foundations) and `5` (Combinatorics) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Open conjectures use `by` + `sorry` (multi-line format: `:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for these open problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- [x] Variant is named `erdos_919.variants.aleph_1` (correct naming pattern).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining the modification (chromatic number exactly `‚ĄĶ‚āĀ` instead of `‚ĄĶ‚āā`).

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č§`, `<`, `‚Üí`, `‚ÜĒ`, `‚Ąē`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**One issue found:** The AMS attribute codes are in descending order (`AMS 5 3`) on both theorems (lines 49 and 71). They must be in ascending numeric order: `AMS 3 5`. The linter `linter.style.ams_attribute` will flag this with: *"The AMS tags should be ordered as AMS 3 5"*.

All other checklist items pass. The formalization is well-structured, the `answer()` usage correctly matches the yes/no phrasing of the problem, the variant is properly named and documented, and the module docstring provides good mathematical context with a bibliography entry.

    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 919
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/919](https://www.erdosproblems.com/919)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThis question was inspired by a theorem of Babai, that if $G$ is a graph on a
    25‚Üíwell-ordered set with chromatic number $\geq \aleph_0$ there is a subgraph on
    26‚Üívertices with order-type $\omega$ with chromatic number $\aleph_0$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Hajnal showed this does not generalise to higher cardinals ‚ÄĒ they
    29‚Üíconstructed a graph on $\omega_1^2$ with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ such that
    30‚Üíevery strictly smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíA similar construction produces a graph on $\omega_2^2$ with chromatic number
    33‚Üí$\aleph_2$ such that every smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_1$.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results in chromatic number theory_.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Cardinal Ordinal
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos919
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/--
    43‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 919 [Er69b]:
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíIs there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
    46‚Üínumber $\aleph_2$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly smaller
    47‚Üíorder type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_919 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
    52‚Üí      (G : SimpleGraph V),
    53‚Üí      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
    54‚Üí        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
    55‚Üí      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 2 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
    56‚Üí      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
    57‚Üí        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <
    58‚Üí          Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚Üí
    59‚Üí        Nonempty ((G.induce S).Coloring ‚Ąē)) := by
    60‚Üí  sorry
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/--
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 919, variant [Er69b]:
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíWhat if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíIs there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
    68‚Üínumber exactly $\aleph_1$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly
    69‚Üísmaller order type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
    70‚Üí-/
    71‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
    72‚Üítheorem erdos_919.variants.aleph_1 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    73‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
    74‚Üí      (G : SimpleGraph V),
    75‚Üí      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
    76‚Üí        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
    77‚Üí      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 1 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
    78‚Üí      (‚ąÉ (őĪ : Type), #őĪ ‚Č§ aleph 1 ‚ąß Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ)) ‚ąß
    79‚Üí      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
    80‚Üí        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <
    81‚Üí          Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚Üí
    82‚Üí        Nonempty ((G.induce S).Coloring ‚Ąē)) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry

    85‚Üíend Erdos919
    86‚Üí

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 919

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/919](https://www.erdosproblems.com/919)

This question was inspired by a theorem of Babai, that if $G$ is a graph on a
well-ordered set with chromatic number $\geq \aleph_0$ there is a subgraph on
vertices with order-type $\omega$ with chromatic number $\aleph_0$.

ErdŇĎs and Hajnal showed this does not generalise to higher cardinals ‚ÄĒ they
constructed a graph on $\omega_1^2$ with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ such that
every strictly smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$.

A similar construction produces a graph on $\omega_2^2$ with chromatic number
$\aleph_2$ such that every smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_1$.

[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results in chromatic number theory_.
-/

open SimpleGraph Cardinal Ordinal

namespace Erdos919

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 919 [Er69b]:

Is there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
number $\aleph_2$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly smaller
order type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
theorem erdos_919 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
      (G : SimpleGraph V),
      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 2 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <
          Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚Üí
        Nonempty ((G.induce S).Coloring ‚Ąē)) := by
  sorry

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 919, variant [Er69b]:

What if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?

Is there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
number exactly $\aleph_1$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly
smaller order type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
theorem erdos_919.variants.aleph_1 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
      (G : SimpleGraph V),
      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 1 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÉ (őĪ : Type), #őĪ ‚Č§ aleph 1 ‚ąß Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <
          Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚Üí
        Nonempty ((G.induce S).Coloring ‚Ąē)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos919

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 919

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/919](https://www.erdosproblems.com/919)

This question was inspired by a theorem of Babai, that if $G$ is a graph on a
well-ordered set with chromatic number $\geq \aleph_0$ there is a subgraph on
vertices with order-type $\omega$ with chromatic number $\aleph_0$.

ErdŇĎs and Hajnal showed this does not generalise to higher cardinals ‚ÄĒ they
constructed a graph on $\omega_1^2$ with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ such that
every strictly smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$.

A similar construction produces a graph on $\omega_2^2$ with chromatic number
$\aleph_2$ such that every smaller subgraph has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_1$.

[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results in chromatic number theory_.
-/

open SimpleGraph Cardinal Ordinal

namespace Erdos919

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 919 [Er69b]:

Is there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
number $\aleph_2$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly smaller
order type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
theorem erdos_919 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
      (G : SimpleGraph V),
      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 2 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <
          Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚Üí
        Nonempty ((G.induce S).Coloring ‚Ąē)) := by
  sorry

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 919, variant [Er69b]:

What if instead we ask for $G$ to have chromatic number $\aleph_1$?

Is there a graph on a well-ordered set of order type $\omega_2 \cdot \omega_2$ with chromatic
number exactly $\aleph_1$ such that every subgraph induced on a subset of strictly
smaller order type has chromatic number $\leq \aleph_0$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 3]
theorem erdos_919.variants.aleph_1 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : LinearOrder V) (_ : IsWellOrder V (¬∑ < ¬∑))
      (G : SimpleGraph V),
      Ordinal.type ((¬∑ < ¬∑) : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop) =
        Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) * Cardinal.ord (aleph 2) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type), Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ) ‚Üí aleph 1 ‚Č§ #őĪ) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÉ (őĪ : Type), #őĪ ‚Č§ aleph 1 ‚ąß Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ)) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ (S : Set V),
        Ordinal.type (Subrel (¬∑ < ¬∑) S) <

end Erdos919
